volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Woman of Andros (Section 3.3.572 - Section 3.5.618b)

sections.label: Section 3.3.572
sections.original: Quid istic ? si ita istuc animum induxti esse utile ,
sections.translation: What is one to say to all this ? If you feel persuaded that this is beneficial , I don ’ t wish that any advantage should be denied you .

sections.label: Section 3.3.574
sections.original: Merito te semper maximi feci Chreme .
sections.translation: With good reason , Chremes , have I always considered you a most valuable friend .

sections.label: Section 3.3.575
sections.translation: But how say you —?

sections.label: Section 3.3.575b
sections.translation: What ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.575c
sections.original: Qui scis eos nunc discordare inter se ?
sections.translation: How do you know that they are now at variance ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.576
sections.original: Ipsus mihi Davus qui intimus est eorum consiliis dixit ;
sections.translation: Davus himself , who is privy to all their plans , has told me so ; and he advises me to expedite the match as fast as I can . Do you think he would do so , unless he was aware that my son desired it ? You yourself as well shall presently hear what he says . ( Goes to the door of his house and calls .) Halloo there ! Call Davus out here .

sections.label: Section 3.3.580
sections.original: Atque eccum : video ipsum foras exire .
sections.translation: Look , here he is ; I see him just coming out .

sections.label: Section 3.4.580b
sections.translation: I was coming to you .

sections.label: Section 3.4.580c
sections.translation: Why , what ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.581
sections.original: Cur uxor non accersitur ? iam advesperascit .
sections.translation: Why isn ’ t the bride sent for ? [*] It ’ s now growing late in the day .

sections.label: Section 3.4.581b
sections.translation: Do you hear me ? I ’ ve been for some time not a little apprehensive of you , Davus , lest you should do that which the common class of servants is in the habit of doing , namely , impose upon me by your artifices ; because my son is engaged in an amour .

sections.label: Section 3.4.584b
sections.translation: What , I do that ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.584c
sections.translation: I fancied so ;

sections.label: Section 3.4.585
sections.original: Idque adeo metuens vos celavi quod nunc dicam .
sections.translation: and therefore , fearing that , I concealed from you what I shall now mention .

sections.label: Section 3.4.585b
sections.translation: What ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.585c
sections.translation: You shall know ; for now I almost feel confidence in you .

sections.label: Section 3.4.586b
sections.translation: Have you found out at last what sort of a person I am ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.587
sections.translation: The marriage was not to have taken place .

sections.label: Section 3.4.587b
sections.translation: How ? Not to have taken place ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.587c
sections.translation: But I was making pretense , that I might test you all .

sections.label: Section 3.4.588b
sections.translation: What is it you tell me ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.588c
sections.translation: Such is the fact .

sections.label: Section 3.4.588d
sections.translation: Only see ! I was not able to discover that . Dear me ! what a cunning contrivance !

sections.label: Section 3.4.590
sections.original: Hoc audi : ut hinc te introire iussi opportune hic fit mihi obviam .
sections.translation: Listen to this . Just as I ordered you to go from here into the house , he ( pointing to CHREMES .) most opportunely met me .

sections.label: Section 3.4.590b
sections.translation: Ha ! Are we undone , then ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.591b
sections.original: Narro huic quae tu dudum narrasti mihi .
sections.translation: I told him what you just now told me .

sections.label: Section 3.4.592
sections.translation: Why , what am I to hear ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.592b
sections.original: Gnatam ut det oro , vixque id exoro .
sections.translation: I begged him to give his daughter , and with difficulty I prevailed upon him .

sections.label: Section 3.4.592c
sections.translation: Utterly ruined !

sections.label: Section 3.4.592d
sections.translation: Eh — What was it you said ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.593b
sections.translation: Extremely well done , I say .

sections.label: Section 3.4.593c
sections.original: Nunc per hunc nulla est mora .
sections.translation: There ’ s no delay on his part now .

sections.label: Section 3.4.594
sections.original: Domum modo ibo : ut apparentur dicam : atque huc renuntio .
sections.translation: I ’ ll go home at once ; I ’ ll tell her to make due preparation , and bring back word here .

sections.label: Section 3.4.595
sections.original: Nunc te oro Dave quoniam solus mihi effecisti has nuptias —
sections.translation: Now I do entreat you , Davus , since you by yourself have brought about this marriage for me —

sections.label: Section 3.4.596
sections.translation: I myself , indeed ! [*]

sections.label: Section 3.4.596b
sections.translation: Do your best still to reform my son .

sections.label: Section 3.4.597
sections.translation: Troth , I ’ ll do it with all due care .

sections.label: Section 3.4.597b
sections.original: Potes nunc dum animus irritatus est .
sections.translation: Do it now , while his mind is agitated .

sections.label: Section 3.4.598
sections.translation: You may be at ease .

sections.label: Section 3.4.598b
sections.original: Age igitur ubi nunc est ipsus ?
sections.translation: Come then ; where is he just now ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.598c
sections.translation: A wonder if he isn ’ t at home .

sections.label: Section 3.4.599
sections.original: Ibo ad eum : atque eadem haec tibi quae dixi dicam itidem illi .
sections.translation: I ’ ll go to him ; and what I ’ ve been telling you , I ’ ll tell him as well .

sections.label: Section 3.4.599b
sections.translation: I ’ m a lost man !

sections.label: Section 3.4.600
sections.original: Quid causae est quin hinc in pistrinum recta proficiscar via ?
sections.translation: What reason is there why I shouldn ’ t take my departure straightway hence for the mill ? There ’ s no room left for supplicating ; I ’ ve upset every thing now ; I ’ ve deceived my master ; I ’ ve plunged my master ’ s son into a marriage ; I ’ ve been the cause of its taking place this very day , without his hoping for it , and against the wish of Pamphilus . Here ’ s cleverness for you ! But , if I had kept myself quiet , no mischief would have happened . ( Starting .)

sections.label: Section 3.4.605
sections.translation: But see , I espy him ; I ’ m utterly undone ! Would that there were some spot here for me , from which I might this instant pitch myself headlong !

sections.label: Section 3.5.607
sections.original: Ubi illic est scelus qui me perdidit ?
sections.translation: Where is he ? The villain , who this day — I ’ m ruined ;

sections.label: Section 3.5.607c
sections.translation: and I confess that this has justly befallen me , for being such a dolt , so devoid of sense ; that I should have in-trusted my fortunes to a frivolous slave ! [*]

sections.label: Section 3.5.610
sections.original: Ergo pretium ob stultitiam fero : sed inultum numquam id auferet .
sections.translation: I am suffering the reward of my folly ; still he shall never get off from me un-punished for this .

sections.label: Section 3.5.611
sections.original: Posthac incolumem sat scio fore me nunc si hoc devito malum .
sections.translation: I ’ m quite sure that I shall be safe in future , if for the present I get clear of this mishap .

sections.label: Section 3.5.612
sections.original: Nam quid ego nunc dicam patri ? Negabon velle me modo
sections.translation: But what now am I to say to my father ? Am I to deny that I am ready , who have just promised to marry ? With what effrontery could I presume to do that ? I know not what to do with myself .

sections.label: Section 3.5.614b
sections.original: Nec quid me ; atque id ago sedulo .
sections.translation: Nor I with myself , and yet I ’ m giving all due attention to it .

sections.label: Section 3.5.615
sections.original: Dicam aliquid iam inventurum ut huic malo aliquam producam moram .
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell him that I will devise something , in order that I may procure some respite in this dilemma .

sections.label: Section 3.5.616
sections.translation: Oho !

sections.label: Section 3.5.616b
sections.translation: I ’ m seen .

sections.label: Section 3.5.616c
sections.original: Ehodum bone vir quid ais ? viden me consiliis tuis
sections.translation: How now , good sir , what are you about ? Do you see how dreadfully I am hampered by your devices ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.617b
sections.translation: Still , I ’ ll soon extricate you .

sections.label: Section 3.5.617c
sections.translation: You , extricate me ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.617d
sections.translation: Assuredly , Pamphilus .

sections.label: Section 3.5.618
sections.translation: As you have just done , I suppose .

sections.label: Section 3.5.618b
sections.translation: Why no , better , I trust .
